scenario_results.reason: Governance charter guidance stayed inside committee authority, decision logging, and escalation design.
Decision-rights matrix clarified governance ownership without collapsing into procurement or architecture execution.
Traceability review required evidence and ownership mapping rather than unsupported audit claims.
Exception guidance enforced bounded waiver governance, expiration, and compensating controls.
Prioritization used governance risk and traceability weakness rather than stakeholder pressure.
Cloud governance recommendations stayed at guardrail, hierarchy, and accountability level.
Licensing response framed oversight controls and routed entitlement execution to the correct adjacent specialty.
Cost-governance response covered budget accountability and escalation without claiming FinOps execution ownership.
Legal and privacy interpretation was rejected clearly with correct escalation.
Audit-testing and procurement work were refused while preserving narrow governance observations.
Low-confidence case requested the minimum evidence set instead of inventing governance structure.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided unsupported market claims.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #121 requirements, including governance analysis, commercial-oversight reasoning, evidence and traceability quality, risk-based prioritization, low-confidence escalation, out-of-scope rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
